workbench.fatalBody: The workbench failed to initialize. Please check the console for details.
description: A Local Workbench for testing SharePoint Framework (SPFx) web parts without deploying to SharePoint
Start SPFx Serve and Open Workbench
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.title: SPFx Local Workbench
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.serveUr: The URL where the SPFx serve is running
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.autoOpe: Automatically open the workbench when starting serve
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.serveCo:
The command to run when starting the SPFx serve from the extension. Common options:
- `heft start --clean --nobrowser`, `heft start --clean --nobrowser --locales=fr-fr`, `npm run start`
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.statusB: Action to perform when clicking the SPFx Workbench status bar button
$(fluentui-testbeakersolid) Start serve and open the Local Workbench panel
Action to perform when clicking the SPFx Storybook status bar button
$(fluentui-teststep) Start serve and open the SPFx Storybook panel
Controls the display style of the SPFx status bar buttons
Show icon and text label on each status bar button
Show icon only on each status bar button
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.theme.c: Select the theme for the workbench
Dark Teal - Microsoft's dark teal theme
Dark Blue - Microsoft's dark blue theme
Custom - Use a custom theme ID
Custom theme name when `#spfxLocalWorkbench.theme.current#` is set to `Custom`. Must match a name defined in `#spfxLocalWorkbench.theme.custom#` - case sensitive
Custom themes in addition to the default Microsoft themes. Each theme should have `name` and `palette` properties
Whether this theme is a dark/inverted theme
Optional URI for a background image
Fluent UI theme palette. Only themePrimary is required — unspecified slots fall back to the default Fluent UI blue palette values (and probably look janky as heck).
Accent color, often the same as themePrimary
Darkest shade of the primary ramp
Dark shade of the primary ramp
Mid-light shade of the primary ramp
Light shade of the primary ramp
Lighter shade of the primary ramp
Lightest shade of the primary ramp
Page background color (required for dark/inverted themes)
Lighter tertiary neutral, used for borders
Page/canvas background (white for light themes, dark for inverted)
Primary text on the page background
Body background, typically same as primaryBackground
Body text color, typically same as primaryText
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.context: SharePoint page context for the mock environment (mirrors SPFx `context.pageContext` structure). Additional properties can be added as needed
Absolute URL of the site collection
Whether the user is an anonymous guest
Current culture code (e.g., en-US, de-DE, fr-FR)
Whether NoScript is enabled on the site
Whether this is SharePoint Online (vs. on-premises)
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.storybo: Port for the Storybook dev server
Automatically generate stories from SPFx component manifests
Glob pattern for discovering custom story files in the SPFx project
Automatically generate story variants for each supported locale
Enable auto-generated documentation pages for stories in Storybook
Automatically install Storybook dependencies without prompting
Controls the Storybook manager UI theme. Requires restarting the Storybook panel to take effect.
Derives the Storybook manager theme from your active VS Code color theme
Uses the Peacock extension color for accent and bar colors, blended with the VS Code theme. Falls back to matchVsCode if no Peacock color is set.
Always use Storybook's built-in light theme
Always use Storybook's built-in dark theme
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.proxy.e: Enable the API proxy system. When disabled, HTTP client calls make real fetch() requests instead of routing through the mock rule engine. Requires closing and reopening the workbench to take effect.
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.proxy.m: Intercept API calls and return mock responses based on configured rules
Forward all API calls to their real endpoints
Serve existing mock rules and capture unmatched requests for stub generation
Path to the Mock rules configuration file (relative to workspace root)
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.proxy.d: Default simulated latency in milliseconds for Mock responses
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.proxy.f: HTTP status code returned when no Mock rule matches in mock mode
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.proxy.l: Log all proxied API requests to the 'SPFx API Proxy' output channel
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.proxy.a: URL patterns allowed in passthrough mode (empty = all)
configuration.spfxLocalWorkbench.proxy.s: When in record mode, also serve existing mock rules for matched requests
extension.notSpfx: This does not appear to be an SPFx project
serve.title: SPFx Serve
serve.timedout: SPFx serve did not start in time. Open workbench anyway?

record.noRequests: No unmatched requests were recorded. All requests may have matched existing rules.

recording.inProgress: Recording API requests. Web parts are being refreshed. Select the status bar or run this command again to stop and generate Mock rules.
workbench.noComponents: No web parts or extensions found in this SPFx project.
